The models in question are defined by their prior distributions: M₁ follows a Beta(1.0, 1.0) prior, while M₂ follows a Beta(0.5, 0.5) prior. The task at hand is to calculate the Bayes factor BF₁₂, which is given by the ratio of the probability of the data under each model, p(x|M₁) and p(x|M₂). You have n = 10 data points for a simple linear regression Y = A + B·x + ε,
where ε ~ N(0, σ²) i.i.d.

Summary statistics:
  n        = 10
  Σxᵢ      = 298.3    (x̄ = 29.83)
  Σyᵢ      = 602.6
  Σxᵢ²     = 10744.97
  Σxᵢyᵢ    = 21419.55

(a) Compute the OLS estimators:
    B̂ = [Σxᵢyᵢ − n·x̄·ȳ] / [Σxᵢ² − n·x̄²]  =  Sxy / Sxx
    Â = ȳ − B̂·x̄
(b) Compute the residual variance  s² = SSR / (n−2),
    where SSR = Σ(yᵢ − Â − B̂·xᵢ)².
(c) Compute the 95% confidence interval for B using the t-distribution
    with df = n−2:  B̂ ± t_{n-2, 0.025} · se(B̂),
    where se(B̂) = √(s² / Sxx).

Report: A_hat, B_hat, s2, B_lower, B_upper
